title: phegopteris oppositifolia
intro: Phegopteris oppositifolia, also known as the oppositely-arranged fern or maiden's hair fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris oppositifolia include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
